presentationFlow.title: Azure Government context
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Same integrated model as commercial, running inside Azure Government. FedRAMP-authorized SIEM, G5 Copilot, GCC compliance boundary.
presentationFlow.gccNote: Defender portal availability in GCC is on a separate rollout schedule.

presentationFlow.title: Discovery
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Audit searches + AI Risk Signals workbook give baseline. Validate IRM and Copilot availability in the readiness scripts before demoing.

presentationFlow.title: Copilot DLP (label-based GA; prompt SIT varies)
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Label-based Copilot DLP is GA in GCC — safe to demo unconditionally. Prompt SIT blocking is public preview commercial; validate tenant availability in GCC before demoing live.
presentationFlow.gccNote: Check feature rollout timing for prompt SIT blocking.

presentationFlow.title: Shadow AI
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Same three layers. Endpoint DLP requires Defender for Endpoint on device. Browser Data Security in Edge for Business available in GCC.

presentationFlow.title: Insider Risk adaptive bridge
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Risky AI Usage IRM template availability in GCC can vary. Data Leaks and Departing User templates are more broadly available. Adaptive enforcement loop works identically when IRM signals flow.
presentationFlow.gccNote: IRM feature rollout in GCC may lag commercial.

presentationFlow.title: Sentinel unified pane
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Sentinel GA in GCC via portal.azure.us. Same 3 connectors, 7 rules, 2 workbooks, IRM auto-triage playbook. Defender portal experience rolling out separately.

presentationFlow.title: The integrated loop
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Same 6-step cycle. Cross-signal correlation fires when both sides flow.

presentationFlow.title: GCC-specific follow-ups
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Microsoft Purview Content Hub solution (available in GCC). DSPM for AI when rollout reaches your tenant. Defender portal onboarding when GCC rollout is available. Compliance Manager FedRAMP High template.
showInPortal: Purview > Solutions > DSPM for AI (when available)

scenarios.name: Label-based Copilot block (GA — reliable GCC demo)
scenarios.description: Label-based Copilot DLP is GA; works identically in GCC.
steps: Open M365 Copilot in GCC tenant as rtorres
Ask Copilot to summarize the Q4 Financial Forecast (labeled Highly Confidential > AI Blocked from External Tools)
Copilot refuses with label-based policy message
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Reliable GCC demo path — label-based DLP is the GA control
manualPrep: Copilot for GCC license assigned to rtorres

scenarios.name: Shadow AI paste block (GCC)
scenarios.description: Device-layer block on paste to external AI sites — works in GCC identically to commercial.
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Same block experience, GCC Sentinel workspace

scenarios.name: GCC feature validation
scenarios.description: Before demo day, confirm which features are available in your specific GCC tenant.
steps: Run all three readiness scripts (Copilot, Shadow AI, Sentinel)
Check Purview > DLP > location picker for Copilot availability
Check Purview > Insider Risk > templates for Risky AI Usage availability
Check Sentinel > Content Hub for Microsoft Purview solution
Check security.microsoft.com for Sentinel node (Defender portal availability)
Document which features are demo-ready vs. pending per-tenant
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Presenter knows which scenarios work live in this GCC tenant

userExperienceTable.scenario: Paste into Copilot chat with SSN
userExperienceTable.systemBehavior: Copilot block works if prompt SIT DLP is available in your GCC tenant — validate first.

userExperienceTable.scenario: Ask Copilot to summarize Highly Confidential file
userExperienceTable.systemBehavior: Label-based block — GA, works reliably in GCC.

userExperienceTable.scenario: Paste into ChatGPT from managed GCC device
userExperienceTable.systemBehavior: Endpoint DLP blocks. Sentinel incident on portal.azure.us.

userExperienceTable.scenario: IRM Risky AI alert + Copilot DLP block same user in 4h
userExperienceTable.systemBehavior: Cross-signal correlation rule fires when both sides flowing. IRM availability in GCC affects this.
PVAISec-LabelDowngrade rule fires in GCC identically.
Normal response — sanctioned GCC Copilot works.
